sources.citation: MDN Web Docs. HTML Microdata and Schema.org.

learning_objectives: L1: Define Shadow DOM, custom elements, and HTML templates as Web Components standards.
L1: Identify structured data formats: JSON-LD, Microdata, RDFa.
L1: State the purpose of template, slot, and portal elements.
L2: Explain how Shadow DOM achieves CSS and JS encapsulation.
L2: Describe how JSON-LD enables rich search results.
L2: Explain how lazy loading and resource hints affect performance.
L2: Compare picture and srcset for responsive images.
L3: Build a reusable Web Component with encapsulated styles and attribute-based API.
L3: Audit a page for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance and produce a prioritised remediation plan.

concepts.title: Custom Elements and Shadow DOM
concepts.explanation: (1) Custom Elements: class MyCard extends HTMLElement; customElements.define('my-card',MyCard). (2) Shadow DOM: isolated subtree — internal CSS doesn't leak; external CSS can't override. (3) Templates: <template> inert until cloned. <slot>: named insertion points.
concepts.worked_example:
class TooltipButton extends HTMLElement { connectedCallback() { const s=this.attachShadow({mode:'open'}); s.innerHTML=`<style>button{background:teal}</style><button><slot>Click</slot></button>`; } }
customElements.define('tooltip-button',TooltipButton);

concepts.title: srcset, sizes, and picture
concepts.explanation: srcset: multiple resolutions — browser picks best. <picture>: art direction — different crops or formats per breakpoint.

concepts.title: Resource Hints
concepts.explanation: <link rel='preload' as='font'>: fetch critical early. <link rel='preconnect'>: TCP/TLS early (~100-300ms saved). loading='lazy': defer until near viewport. fetchpriority='high': prioritise LCP image.
concepts.worked_example: Preload hero + preconnect CDN + fetchpriority='high' on LCP img: LCP 3.2s→1.4s.

concepts.title: JSON-LD and Schema.org
concepts.explanation: JSON-LD (Google recommended): <script type='application/ld+json'>. Schema.org: Article, Product, Recipe, FAQPage. Enables rich results. Validate with Google Rich Results Test.

concepts.title: WCAG 2.1 AA — POUR Principles
concepts.explanation: POUR: Perceivable, Operable, Understandable, Robust. Key AA: 1.1.1 alt text; 1.4.3 contrast 4.5:1; 2.1.1 keyboard accessible; 2.4.7 focus visible; 4.1.2 name/role/value.
concepts.worked_example: Audit: (1) missing alt → add. (2) icon button no label → aria-label='Close'. (3) <div> dropdown → role='listbox'. (4) red-only error → aria-invalid+aria-describedby. (5) modal → focus trap.

concepts.title: Progressive Enhancement
concepts.explanation: Build core in plain HTML first. Layer CSS, then JS. HTML validation (W3C Validator): catches missing closing tags, invalid nesting, duplicate IDs.
concepts.worked_example: Form with JS validation: without JS, native HTML5 constraints (required, type='email') still work. JS is enhancement, not dependency.
